explanation_used:
1. **Opening statement**: This neuron strongly responds to **text that explains, elaborates on, or provides a consequence of a preceding idea** (such as clarifying a point with "just that", detailing how something is done, or stating a resulting action introduced by phrases like "do just that," "is how," or "since").

2. **Domain coverage**: The pattern appears across diverse English-language domains including politics, business, technology, healthcare, and entertainment. It activates on follow-up clauses that specify methods, outcomes, or definitions, often signaled by conjunctions or demonstratives ("that," "since," "how," "just") linking back to a prior statement. Examples also include non-English text (e.g., Czech) when it contains similar explanatory structures.

3. **Contrast with low-activation examples**: The low-activation texts are primarily sentence fragments, headlines, or isolated statements that introduce a topic but lack explanatory or consequential elaboration. They are often the *setup* without the *follow-through*. What distinguishes the high group is the presence of a logical connector or anaphoric reference ("that," "it," "since") tying the clause to a prior context, creating a sense of continuation or implementation.

4. **Closing interpretation**: This neuron appears to detect linguistic markers of elaboration and consequence within a discourse. It fires on clauses that serve to specify, justify, or enact a previously mentioned concept, acting as a detector for explanatory continuity rather than for any specific topic.

results.plain_text: Steamboat Resort celebrates 60th anniversary with a drone show and more this weekend  On Jan. 12, Steamboat Resort turned 60, celebrating six decades since a locally owned ski hill called Storm Mountain opened with one chairlift and a poma.  To commemorate the occasion, the resort is hosting an event like none other: a drone show.  “We do fireworks quite often, it’s not the most special thing,” said Morgan Bast, director of marketing at Steamboat Ski and Resort Corp. “We’re all about storytelling and sharing the story of Steamboat with people. This is a very new way to do that.”  The show will kick off at 5:30 p.m. on Saturday, Jan. 21, in Steamboat Square.  There will be a torchlight parade with Snow Sports School instructors, as well as a performance by the Steamboat Skating Club on Skeeter’s Rink.  Anywhere in Steamboat Square, or the bottom of Stampede is a prime spot to catch the drone show, which will feature 150 drones telling the tale of Steamboat Resort over about 10 minutes. The drones will perform a synchronized display of images composed to music and aided by a commentary that will take viewers through important moments in the history of Steamboat Resort.  Bast said it should be an immersive experience for attendees.  “Just listening to the music going along to the drone show has sent a couple of us in the office into tears,” she said. “It’s so powerful and moving. You’ll hear some fun jingles from over the years.”  There will be complimentary cupcakes, hot cocoa, a champagne toast and custom Coca-Cola glasses.  After the drone show, Steamboat Square becomes a party. Night skiing will begin at 6 p.m., as will a special cosmic skate night at Skeeter’s Rink.  One important thing to keep in mind is that the event is weather dependent. If precipitation keeps the drones grounded, the show will be postponed to Sunday, Jan. 22, or Monday, Jan. 23.  Prospective viewers should keep an eye on Steamboat.com, or the Steamboat Resort app, which will alert nearby users if the event time has been changed.  Shelby Reardon is the assistant editor at the Steamboat Pilot & Today. To reach her, call 970-871-4253, email sreardon@SteamboatPilot.com or follow her on Twitter @ByShelbyReardon.  Support Local Journalism  Support Local Journalism  Readers around Steamboat and Routt County make the Steamboat Pilot & Today’s work possible. Your financial contribution supports our efforts to deliver quality, locally relevant journalism.  Now more than ever, your support is critical to help us keep our community informed about the evolving coronavirus pandemic and the impact it is having locally. Every contribution, however large or small, will make a difference.  Each donation will be used exclusively for the development and creation of increased news coverage.

results.actual_text: AFP from his home in Pula village near the volcano. “There was like a fire on the mouth of the volcano, which lasted around one to two minutes
results.plain_text: MANILA, June 3, 2024 (AFP) – The alert level for a Philippine volcano was raised Monday after an “explosive eruption” sent a plume of ash, gas and steam five kilometres (three miles) into the sky, the volcanology agency said.  Mount Kanlaon on the central island of Negros erupted shortly before 7:00 pm (1100 GMT), prompting warnings for nearby residents to wear facemasks due the threat of volcanic gases and falling ash.  “When it erupted we heard a thunder-like sound,” Ethan Asentista-Khoo, 35, told AFP from his home in Pula village near the volcano.  “There was like a fire on the mouth of the volcano, which lasted around one to two minutes. I didn’t see any lava or rocks coming out.”  The Philippines is located in the seismically active Pacific “Ring of Fire” that hosts more than half of the world’s volcanoes.  Kanlaon is one of 24 active volcanoes in the archipelago nation.  Eruptions can be deadly, with pyroclastic and lahar flows as well as ashfall posing hazards to communities surrounding the volcano.

results.actual_text: Trump, though she said she accepts the US intelligence community's assessment that concluded just that. Speaking at the Aspen Security Forum, Nielsen created some confusion when she seemed
results.plain_text: DHS Secretary Kirstjen Nielsen on Thursday declined to say she agrees that Russian election interference was designed to help President Donald Trump, though she said she accepts the US intelligence community's assessment that concluded just that.  Speaking at the Aspen Security Forum, Nielsen created some confusion when she seemed to deny that the Russian attacks on US elections were designed to help Trump. She was responding to a question from NBC's Peter Alexander about her May comments to CNN's Manu Raju that she had not seen that conclusion.  "I haven't seen any evidence that the attempts to interfere in our election infrastructure was to favor a particular political party," Nielsen said Thursday. "I think what we've seen on the foreign influence side is they were attempting to intervene and cause chaos on both sides. ... I think the overall purpose is to sow discord and get us all to fight against each other rather than understand who the enemy is."  Pressed by Alexander that the intelligence community concluded it was to help Trump, Nielsen said "certain political parties ...more than others" were attacked and that DHS was focused on preparing for the future.  After her comments in May, DHS later clarified that Nielsen agrees with the US intelligence assessment. That joint statement from the full US intelligence community in 2017 concluded that Russian President Vladimir Putin ordered an influence campaign during the 2016 US presidential election with goals that included harming Hillary Clinton's candidacy. The Russian government "developed a clear preference for President-elect Trump" and tried to help his election chances, the assessment stated.  But later in Thursday's interview, Nielsen reiterated she agrees with the intelligence assessment.  "I do not disagree. I agree completely, I've said that many times, with the intel assessment," Nielsen said. "What I'm trying to focus my comments on is the fact that they have the capability and the will. And what the role of DHS is, is to prepare for them to continue to try to use both foreign influence and direct attacks on infrastructure to effect the results of our election."  CNN's Jim Sciutto followed up by asking Nielsen to clarify where she stood, and whether she would state unequivocally that Russia's "intention was to help Donald Trump and disadvantage Hillary Clinton."  "I agree with the intel community's assessment, full stop," Nielsen replied. "The intel community is made up of professionals who do this for a living, who are dedicated public servants. I absolutely believe their assessment."  Related Content  DHS secretary dodges saying Russian interference was to help Trump Trump dodges question on Russian interference Kirstjen Nielsen sworn in as DHS secretary Kirstjen Nielsen sworn in as DHS secretary Kirstjen Nielsen sworn in as DHS secretary Kirstjen Nielsen sworn in as DHS secretary Kirstjen Nielsen sworn in as DHS secretary Kirstjen Nielsen sworn in as DHS secretary Kirstjen Nielsen sworn in as DHS secretary Kirstjen Nielsen sworn in as DHS secretary

results.actual_text: Barcelona return to La Liga action as they take on Real Betis on Sunday.
results.plain_text: Barcelona return to La Liga action as they take on Real Betis on Sunday.  Ernesto Valverde's men sit pretty at the top of the table and can go 10 points clear of Atletico Madrid with a victory.  Barca made light work of Lyon in midweek in the Champions League and will be eyeing another success in this one.  Betis meanwhile have had a decent campaign to date and are only four points off the European spots.  Can they cause an upset and beat the league leaders?  Follow all the action from the La Liga clash below. Kick-off is at 7:45pm (UK).  Real Betis: Pau Lopez, Joaquin, Mandi, Bartra, Sidnei, Tello, William, Guardado, Canales, Lo Celso, Jese  Barcelona: Ter Stegen, Roberto, Pique, Lenglet, Alba, Busquets, Arthur, Rakitic, Vidal, Messi, Suarez The Betis defenders are struggling to get the ball clear at the minute and Rakitic picks up the ball in a good position on the edge of the box, but he takes too long to decide what he wants to do and Bartra is able to dispossess him and clear the danger. Suarez has dropped deep into midfield to receive the ball and he plays a brilliant ball over the top of Betis' defenders to pick out Alba, but the left back is well offside and the attack comes to an end. Guardado gets the ball on the left wing this time and puts a good cross into the box and Canales makes a darting run between the defenders to get on the end of it, but his header is well saved by Ter Stegen. Tello takes the ball to the byline and squares his cross across the six-yard box and into a dangerous position. Lo Celso comes close to getting on the end of it, but it's just out of his reach and Barcelona are able to get it clear. Barcelona have another free-kick in a similar position to the one that led to the goal. Messi is stood over it again and he tries to go for the near post this time, but Lopez gets across his line well to catch the effort. Vidal play a brilliant pass to Messi who has just pulled away from his defender as he makes his run into the box, but his touch lets him down and Sidnei is able to get back to win the ball back. Alba gets the ball in a lot of space down the left wing and tries to play Suarez in quickly, but he overhits the pass and Lopez is able to come off his line to collect it. Betis are straight on the attack and are trying to get a quick equaliser, but Barcelona seem to have found their rhythm in the game now and are closing them down faster now when they get into the final third. MESSI GIVES BARCELONA THE LEAD IN STYLE! Barcelona won a free-kick on the edge of the box and the captain stepped up to take it. He curled it beautifully into the top-left corner and out of the reach of Lopez. 1-0 Barcelona! Guardado receives a yellow card for dissent. Betis have played themselves into trouble a couple of times in their own half with a couple of poor passes, which is allowing Barcelona to pile a bit of pressure on the home side. GOOD CHANCE FOR JESE! Joaquin made a great run down the right wing and switched play to Guardado on the left. He put a cross into the box with his first touch and Jese couldn't get the contact that he needed on the ball to cause the keeper any problems as it goes wide of the far post. Barcelona have made a nervous start to this game and don't look comfortable whenever the Betis players start moving forward to attack. They sit back when Tello gets the ball and he takes the shot from outside the box, but it's an easy save for Ter Stegen. Betis are keeping possession well in their own half and Bartra thinks he sees an opening up front so tries to loop the ball up to Joaquin, but his pass is overhit and rolls out of play. Messi picks up the ball in midfield and plays a good throughball to Alba, who did look offside as the pass was played. Mandi recovered well to track his run though and made an important tackle to put it out of play. A great ball over the top of the Barcelona midfield picks out Tello brilliantly as he pulls away from his defender on the left wing, but his touch to bring down the pass is poor and the ball rolls harmlessly out of play. Betis have made a bright start to the game and are keeping possession well as they move the ball into a dangerous position in the final third, but Barcelona are able to get it clear. There have been a couple of poor passes from the Barcelona players in the opening couple of minutes in the game which is allowing Betis to put them under pressure early on. Betis get the game underway at the Benito Villamarin. The teams are coming out onto the field now, with kick-off just moments away. Setien is looking to become the first Betis manager to record a league double over Barcelona, following their 4-3 win at Camp Nou earlier in the season. Ernesto Valverde also makes just the one change after Barcelona's Champions League win over Lyon on Wednesday, with Vidal coming in to replace Coutinho, who drops down to the bench. Quique Setien makes just one change to the Betis side that beat Celta Vigo in the league last weekend as Tello comes into the team to replace Francis Guererro. BARCELONA SUBS: Jasper Cillessen, Carles Alena, Philippe Coutinho, Malcom, Kevin-Prince Boateng, Nelson Semedo, Samuel Umtiti. BARCELONA STARTING XI (4-4-2): Marc-Andre ter Stegen; Sergi Roberto, Gerard Pique, Clement Lenglet, Jordi Alba; Arturo Vidal, Sergio Busquets, Arthur, Ivan Rakitic; Luis Suarez, Lionel Messi. REAL BETIS SUBS: Joel Robles, Sergio Leon, Wilfried Kaptoum, Diego Lainez, Loren Moron, Zouhair Feddal, Emerson. REAL BETIS STARTING XI (3-4-2-1): Pau Lopez; Aissa Mandi, Marc Bartra, Sidnei; Joaquin, William Carvalho, Andres Guardado, Cristian Tello; Giovani Lo Celso, Sergio Canales; Jese. Betis come into this game having lost just one of their last six matches in the league (W5 D1), although that loss did come in their last home game when they lost 2-1 to Getafe. Barcelona, on the other hand, are currently on the longest unbeaten run in LaLiga at the minute, having gone 15 games without losing in the competition (W12 D3). Hello and welcome to live coverage of the LaLiga game between Real Betis and Barcelona at the Benito Villamarin! No Philippe Coutinho for Barca. The Brazilian has been left out of the starting line-up by Valverde. Vidal starts for the La Liga leaders.

results.actual_text: The Friday night lights of Texas high school football have taken on a mythical quality over the
results.plain_text: The Friday night lights of Texas high school football have taken on a mythical quality over the years, but Thursdays have a storied history of their own. Author Michael Hurd will take part in a live virtual event for the Allen Public Library to discuss the largely unknown history of Black high school football in the state. Hurd’s Thursday Night Lights: The Story of Black High School Football in Texas was named one of the 30 best Texas books for the 2010-19 decade by The Texas Observer and one of the 30 most essential books about Texas by the Houston Chronicle. The live event will be at 7:30 p.m. May 13 at allentx.swagit.com. Former Dallas Morning News columnist James Ragland will moderate the program. Former Dallas Cowboys receiver Tony Hill will also take part. Thursday Night Lights tells the inspiring story of Black high school football in Texas from 1920 to 1970. The segregated high schools in the Prairie View Interscholastic League (the Black counterpart to the University Interscholastic League) included future NFL greats such as “Mean” Joe Greene, Otis Taylor and Charles “Bubba” Smith, who appeared in six Police Academy movies after his All-Pro career. Hurd is also the author of Black College Football, 1892-1992, the only book that comprehensively documents the legacies of football programs at historically Black colleges, and he serves on the selection committee for the Black College Football Hall of Fame. He is a board member for the Writers’ League of Texas, a trustee with the Friends of the Texas Historical Commission and a member of the Texas Institute of Letters. For details about the virtual event, call 214-509-4911.
